purpose: Task 13 source evidence for Gate B: the six COMPLETE published Champions M-A teams selected by the owner-fixed rule. Every EV, nature, item, ability and move below is the publisher's own -- nothing is synthesized.
supersedes: docs/projects/champions/audits/2026-07-21-task13-source-evidence/ (Rutgers Scarlet Classic). That set remains valid as history and its independent review stands; it is no longer the Task 13 construction path, because its Open Team Sheet tier structurally never publishes EVs/natures.

source.sheet_backing_range: IMPORTRANGE of 'Champions M-A Featured!A1:AS400' from spreadsheet 1r6kYCyhnWbBbLfJrYEwB23sPayo2p9lFKil_ZKHlrYA

selection_rule: The first six entries, in sheet table order, in 'Champions M-A Featured Teams' with EVs = Yes, a reachable PokePaste, six complete Pokemon, and declared format gen9championsvgc2026regma. Fixed by the project owner BEFORE any paste was read for construction; the six team IDs were enumerated explicitly and may not be substituted or re-ordered.
selection_rule_verification: Independently confirmed against the sheet's own CSV export: rows 1-6 in table order are PC1102, PC1101, PC1100, PC1099, PC1098, PC1097, each with EVs=Yes. The next row (PC1097+1 = PC1096) is also EVs=Yes, so the cut at six is the rule's count, not an EV-availability artifact.
extraction_rule: Browser get_page_text against each paste's own /raw endpoint -- the canonical Pokemon Showdown paste text, not the rendered HTML view. The frozen .txt files are NORMALIZED, not byte-identical to what the endpoint served: per-line trailing whitespace (a text-extraction artifact of the renderer) was stripped, and the files are written UTF-8, LF-only, with a single trailing newline. No Pokemon, field, value, or ordering was changed. The sha256 values below are over these normalized files, which are the artifact this manifest tracks -- they are NOT a digest of the upstream HTTP response body, and must not be cited as one.
sha256_algorithm: sha256 over the frozen .txt file's exact bytes (UTF-8, LF, single trailing newline)
synthetic_fields: NONE. Species, item, ability, level, EVs, nature and all four moves per Pokemon are the publisher's own published values, transcribed unchanged. No EV/nature synthesis rule is used or needed for this source.
legality_authority: pokemon-showdown validate-team gen9championsvgc2026regma, run against the pinned checkout at f8ac140. Not derived from speciesdata.json/itemdata.json.
not_yet_done: Canonical .txt/.packed team artifacts under showdown_bot/teams/ (Task 13 step 2)
seal_team sealing and content hashes (Task 12 API, Task 13 step 2)
Exact hash disjointness against the frozen coverage set (requires the packed artifacts)
Repo-wide leakage scan, panel/holdout/baseline manifests, CLI data wiring (Task 13 step 3)

selection_proof.provenance: CSV export of the documented gviz endpoint for this sheet+gid, downloaded by the project owner and supplied locally, then frozen here unmodified. NOT agent-fetched: the browser tool was denied navigation to docs.google.com for this endpoint.
selection_proof.independent_corroboration: Before this file was supplied, the reviewing agent independently fetched the SAME gviz endpoint read-only and observed the same ordering of the first seven EVs=Yes rows (PC1102, PC1101, PC1100, PC1099, PC1098, PC1097, then PC1096). The frozen file agrees with that independent observation. Note the earlier fetch returned a model-rendered simplification of the CSV, not its bytes -- this frozen file is the authority for column names and values, and it is what corrected the placement wording in `entries[]`.
selection_proof.parse_method: Python csv.reader over the file as-is. The real header row is at index 2 (rows 0-1 are sheet preamble); load-bearing columns are 'Team ID', 'Team Description', 'Full Name', 'Pokepaste', 'EVs', 'Rank', 'Tournament / Event'. Data rows are those with a non-empty 'Team ID', in file order.
selection_proof.verified_claim: The first six data rows in table order are PC1102, PC1101, PC1100, PC1099, PC1098, PC1097, each with EVs='Yes' and the exact Pokepaste URL frozen in entries[]. The seventh (PC1096) is also EVs='Yes', so the cut at six is the selection rule's own count, not the point at which EV data runs out.
selection_proof.note_on_placement_fields: sheet_rank/sheet_event/sheet_category/sheet_full_name are copied verbatim from this file. An earlier revision of this manifest carried placement strings such as '1st place (gold), Masters' that were an artifact of a model-rendered reading of the CSV; the sheet has no 'Placement' column and no 'Masters' designation. Corrected against these bytes.

blindness_attestation.statement: The six teams were selected blind to this bot's results. The selection rule is positional and mechanical -- the first six table-order entries meeting fixed predicates -- and was fixed and enumerated by the project owner BEFORE any paste was read for construction. No team was chosen, kept, dropped, or re-ordered because of how the bot performs against it; no bot result, win rate, cell exposure, coverage outcome, or Gate B verdict existed for any of these teams at selection time, and none was consulted.
blindness_attestation.verifiable_basis: The rule is reproducible from selection-source-export.csv alone: anyone re-running the documented parse gets the same six IDs in the same order, without reference to any bot artifact. Independence therefore does not rest on the selecting party's own assurance.
blindness_attestation.what_it_does_not_claim: It does not claim archetype-disjointness from the existing panel, nor that the six are unlike teams the bot was tuned against. That is a separate, narrower property; exact hash disjointness and the reference near-duplicate flags are computed later, in Task 13 step 3.
blindness_attestation.spec_basis: APPROVED spec sec 3.4 -- 'the six teams come from a source or curation process that is blind to the bot's own results' and 'the sourcing rationale ... is fixed before any team is ever exposed to the bot'.

format_declaration_proof.why: The frozen pc*-paste.txt files come from each paste's /raw endpoint, which carries ONLY the team text and no format line -- so `declared_format` in entries[] had no frozen evidence behind it. This file supplies it: the per-paste metadata block (title, uploading account, declared format) read from each paste's own HTML page.
format_declaration_proof.method: Browser accessibility-tree read (read_page, depth 1) of each paste page's <aside> metadata block. This is a transcription of rendered text, not a byte capture of the HTTP response body.
format_declaration_proof.observed_uploader_variation: Five pastes are uploaded by the 'VGCPastes' account; PC1098 by 'Guest 20026542'. Recorded as observed; it affects neither the selection rule (which keys on the sheet row) nor legality (which validate-team decides).
format_declaration_proof.relationship_to_legality: This proves what the SOURCE DECLARES, which is the selection rule's format predicate. It is not a legality result -- validate-team remains the sole legality authority and is reported separately.
